input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

### XAML — `AppThemeBinding`

Replace every hardcoded color with an `AppThemeBinding` that supplies a value for `Light` and `Dark`:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">

        <Label Text="Welcome!"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page respects your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#BBBBBB}" />

        <Button Text="Get Started"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#512BD4, Dark=#B39DDB}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

MAUI reads the OS theme at startup **and** listens for changes — no extra wiring needed. When the user toggles system dark mode, every `AppThemeBinding` re-evaluates automatically.

### C# alternative — `SetAppTheme` extension method

The programmatic equivalent uses `SetAppThemeColor` (for colors) or the general `SetAppTheme<T>` (for any property):

```csharp
var page = new ContentPage();
page.SetAppThemeColor(
    ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    light: Colors.White,
    dark:  Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E"));

var heading = new Label { Text = "Welcome!", FontSize = 28 };
heading.SetAppThemeColor(
    Label.TextColorProperty,
    light: Colors.Black,
    dark:  Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0"));

var button = new Button { Text = "Get Started" };
button.SetAppThemeColor(
    Button.BackgroundColorProperty,
    light: Color.FromArgb("#512BD4"),
    dark:  Color.FromArgb("#B39DDB"));
```

For non-color properties use the generic form:

```csharp
label.SetAppTheme<string>(
    Label.TextProperty,
    light: "Light mode active",
    dark:  "Dark mode active");
```

### Scaling up with resource dictionaries

Once you have more than a handful of colors, define them once in `App.xaml` and reference by key:

```xml
<!-- App.xaml -->
<Application.Resources>
    <Color x:Key="PageBg">
        {AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}
    </Color>
</Application.Resources>

<!-- Any page -->
<ContentPage BackgroundColor="{StaticResource PageBg}" />
```

### Detecting the current theme in code

```csharp
AppTheme theme = Application.Current.RequestedTheme;
// Returns AppTheme.Light, AppTheme.Dark, or AppTheme.Unspecified

// React to runtime changes:
Application.Current.RequestedThemeChanged += (s, e) =>
{
    Debug.WriteLine($"Theme changed to {e.RequestedTheme}");
};
```

**Key points:**
- `AppThemeBinding` is reactive — no manual refresh needed when the OS theme changes.
- `SetAppThemeColor` / `SetAppTheme<T>` are the C# equivalents.
- For larger apps, centralize colors in resource dictionaries or `Styles` to avoid repetition.
